title: Return value of nodiscard functions should not be ignored
description: Return value of "nodiscard" functions should not be ignoredAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?If a function is defined with a[[nodiscard]]attribute or if it returns an object which is[[nodiscard]], its return value is very important and should not be silently ignored.Noncompliant code examplestruct [[nodiscard]] ErrorInfo{ /* ... */}; ErrorInfo getStatus(); [[nodiscard]] int getInfo(); void f() { getStatus(); // Noncompliant; we should read the returned struct which is "nodiscard" getInfo(); // Noncompliant; we should read the return value of this "nodiscard" function // ... }Compliant solutionstruct[[nodiscard]] ErrorInfo{ /* ..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Function declared with [[nodiscard]] attribute has its return value ignored. Capture or use the return value.
Function returning nodiscard type has its return value ignored. Assign return value to variable.
Standalone function call ignoring nodiscard return value. Use the returned value in assignment or condition.
Common nodiscard function called without using return value. Assign result to variable or use in expression.
Nodiscard function definition followed by ignored function call. Check if the called function returns nodiscard type.
Nodiscard function return value explicitly cast to void. Remove void cast and use return value appropriately.
Nodiscard function return value intentionally ignored. This defeats the purpose of the nodiscard attribute.
Function with nodiscard return type called without capturing or using the return value.
Function returning nodiscard struct/class type has return value ignored. Capture the returned object.
